name: "Document Request"

title: "[DOC REQUEST]: "
labels: ["documentation", "document-request", "needs-review"]
assignees: ""
---

# Document Request

## 1. Request Summary

Provide a clear summary of the document being requested.

```text
Example:
Create a new README for the /pce folder explaining Prompt Compliance Engineering and how it connects to OPRMT™.
```

---

## 2. Requested Document Type

Select the document type that best matches the request.

- [ ] README file
- [ ] Framework specification
- [ ] Component specification
- [ ] Governance policy
- [ ] Legal or usage-policy document
- [ ] Prompt template
- [ ] Prompt cluster document
- [ ] PCE documentation
- [ ] DOAS™ scoring documentation
- [ ] Certification or curriculum document
- [ ] Automation or script documentation
- [ ] Repository index file
- [ ] Contributor guide
- [ ] Security or maintenance document
- [ ] Other

If other, describe:

```text
Other document type:
```

---

## 3. Recommended Repository Path

Provide the proposed location for the new document.

```text
Example:
/pce/Prompt-Compliance-Engineering-Overview.md
```

If the correct location is unknown, write:

```text
Path unknown - repository specialist should recommend placement.
```

---

## 4. Document Purpose

Explain why this document is needed and what repository problem it solves.

```text
Purpose:
```

---

## 5. Target Audience

Select the primary audience for this document.

- [ ] Repository maintainer
- [ ] Contributor
- [ ] Prompt engineer
- [ ] Student or certification learner
- [ ] Customer or product user
- [ ] Developer or automation builder
- [ ] Legal or compliance reviewer
- [ ] Public GitHub visitor
- [ ] Internal OPRMT™ operator
- [ ] Other

---

## 6. Required Sections

List the sections that should be included in the requested document.

```text
Example:
1. Purpose
2. Scope
3. Definitions
4. Required structure
5. Usage rules
6. Examples
7. Versioning notes
8. Ownership notice
```

---

## 7. Source Material or References

Provide any existing files, notes, issue links, external references, or prior drafts that should be used.

```text
Related sources:
```

If there are no source materials, write:

```text
No source material provided. Create from repository standards and approved OPRMT™ terminology.
```

---

## 8. OPRMT™ Framework Alignment

Does this document need to align with the official OPRMT™ structure?

- [ ] Yes
- [ ] No
- [ ] Unsure

If yes, select the affected component or system area.

- [ ] Objective
- [ ] Parameters
- [ ] Results
- [ ] Method
- [ ] Tools
- [ ] Full OPRMT™ framework sequence
- [ ] Prompt Compliance Engineering
- [ ] DOAS™ scoring
- [ ] Certification system
- [ ] Repository governance
- [ ] Legal or licensing system
- [ ] Other

Describe the required alignment:

```text
Alignment notes:
```

---

## 9. Metadata Requirements

Select the metadata fields that should appear in the document.

- [ ] Title
- [ ] System name
- [ ] Document type
- [ ] Version
- [ ] Status
- [ ] Owner
- [ ] Repository path
- [ ] Created date
- [ ] Last updated date
- [ ] License reference
- [ ] Usage scope
- [ ] Related documents
- [ ] Review status

Recommended metadata block:

```yaml
---
title: "Document Title"
system: "OPRMT™"
document_type: "Guide | Specification | Policy | Template | Index | README"
version: "v1.0.0"
status: "Draft | Review | Approved | Deprecated"
owner: "Michael W. Fleming"
repository_path: "/path/to/file.md"
created_date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
last_updated: "YYYY-MM-DD"
license_reference: "See LICENSE.md"
usage_scope: "Repository documentation and approved implementation"
---
```

---

## 10. Priority Level

Select the request priority.

- [ ] Critical - needed before release or public publishing
- [ ] High - important for repository structure, user clarity, or framework integrity
- [ ] Medium - useful but not blocking
- [ ] Low - optional improvement or future enhancement

---

## 11. Completion Criteria

Define what must be true for this document request to be considered complete.

```text
Example:
- File is created in the correct folder.
- Metadata block is included.
- Document uses official OPRMT™ terminology.
- Internal links are accurate.
- Ownership notice is included.
- README or index file is updated if needed.
```

---

## 13. Risks or Constraints

Identify any risks, constraints, or boundaries for this document.

- [ ] Must not create unsupported legal claims
- [ ] Must not claim accreditation or licensing authority
- [ ] Must not conflict with LICENSE.md
- [ ] Must preserve OPRMT™ ownership language
- [ ] Must not change canonical OPRMT™ terminology
- [ ] Must be suitable for public GitHub visibility
- [ ] Must be internal-only
- [ ] Other

Additional constraints:

```text
Constraints:
```

Proposed title:

# Ownership Notice

OPRMT™ and related framework materials are owned by Michael W. Fleming unless otherwise stated in the repository license, notice, or usage policy.

This document request template supports structured documentation intake, repository governance, framework integrity, contributor clarity, and controlled expansion of the OPRMT™ repository.
